text: This paper analyses the characteristics of modern globalization in the context of Global Value Chains (GVCs) and their impact on countries in Central and Eastern Europe (CEE). We identify the specific challenges that each CEE country faces (such as the risk of the middle-income trap and the risk of being at the low-skilled end of the GVC), and suggest ways of reformulating public policy to sustain economic growth. The key factor is the use of technology to move to higher value-added activities in global production networks. The analysis is based on empirical studies that make use of our own indices measuring the involvement of CEE countries in GVCs as well as an extensive review of the literature.
